zh.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"是Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO和Estevainha Peres的兒子，於1125年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈。他於大約1150年與Constanza Osorio結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安被列為aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro.。他去世於1185年在西班牙可蒙尼達 瓦倫希亞納阿利坎特蒙福特 德爾 希德蒙福特享年60歲，埋葬在西班牙。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"是Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO和Estevainha Peres的儿子，于1125年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈。他于大约1150年与Constanza Osorio结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安被列为aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro.。他去世于1185年在西班牙可蒙尼達 瓦倫希亞納阿利坎特蒙福特 德爾 希德蒙福特享年60岁，埋葬在西班牙。

nameMeanings.name: Rodríguez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Pedro, Raul, Miguel, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Ligia, Catarina, Paulo, Joaquim, Omero, Joao, Wenseslao, Adauto, Albeiro, Godofredo.

Spanish (Rodríguez) and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Rodrigo . Rodriguez (including in the sense 2 below) is the ninth most frequent surname in the US. Compare De Rodriguez and Rodriquez .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Iberian surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Rodiguez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Adriana, Alvaro, Ana Luz, Angel, Armando, Arnulfo.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodriquez
meanings: Spanish (Rodríquez): variant of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). This surname is very rare in Spain.

variants.name: Rodriges
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodreguez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Francisco, Mario, Pedro, Raul.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Ligia, Vasco, Wenceslao, Armanda, Fernandes.

Galician, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): topographic name from castro ‘castle, fortress’ (from Latin castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also De Castro .

Italian and Jewish (from Italy): topographic name for someone who lived by a fortification, from castro ‘fortification’ (see 1 above) or a habitational name for someone from the town of Castro, the center of a Duchy in Lazio (destroyed after its occupation by Papal military forces in 1649). Compare Lo Castro .

variants.name: Alcazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Julio, Rafael, Raul, Ruben, Alicia, Gustavo.

Spanish (Alcázar): habitational name from any of various places, for example in the provinces of Ciudad Real, Cuenca, and Granada, named with the word alcázar ‘citadel’ or ‘palace’ (from Arabic al ‘the’ + qaṣr ‘fortress’, a borrowing of Latin castrum; compare Castro ).

variants.name: De Castro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Armando, Mario, Alfredo, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Ricardo, Rolando, Alvaro.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name for someone from any of various places called with castro ‘castle, fortress’ (see Castro ), with the preposition de ‘of, from’.

variants.name: Castrillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jorge, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Guillermo, Ramon, Alberto, Alejandro, Ana, Benito.
Spanish: habitational name from any of various places, especially in León, named Castrillo, from castrillo ‘fort’, a diminutive of Castro .

variants.name: Castronovo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Santo, Cosimo, Giacomo, Graziella, Sal, Stefano.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Castronovo, from castro ‘fortification’ (see Castro ) + novo ‘new’.

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Hernán
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

variants.name: Hernando
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

en.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" was born in 1125, in Castilla-La Mancha, Spain as the son of Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO and Estevainha Peres. He married Constanza Osorio about 1150. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. in Castilla y León, Spain. He died in 1185, in Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, Spain, at the age of 60, and was buried in Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" est né en 1125, à Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, fils de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO et Estevainha Peres. Il a épousé Constanza Osorio environ 1150. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. à Castilla y León, Espagne. Il est décédé en 1185, à Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, Espagne, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" wurde 1125 in Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien, als Sohn von Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO und Estevainha Peres geboren. Er hat um 1150 Constanza Osorio geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. in Castilla y León, Spanien aufgeführt. Er starb 1185 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, Spanien und wurde in Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" è nato il nel 1125 nel Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna come figlio di Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO e Estevainha Peres. Lui sposato Constanza Osorio circa 1150. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. nel Castilla y León, Spagna. Morì nel 1185, nel Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, Spagna, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Spagna.

ko.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano"(은)는 1125년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차에서 Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO(와)과 Estevainha Peres의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1150년 Constanza Osorio(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o에서 aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro.이었다. 그는 1185년 스페인 코먼이다드 발샤나 앨리캉 몬포트 데 시드 몬포트에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" nasceu em 1125, em Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha filho de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO e Estevainha Peres. Ele casou-se com Constanza Osorio aproximadamente 1150. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. em Castilla y León, Espanha. Ele faleceu em 1185, em Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, Espanha, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" родился в 1125 г. в местности кастилла-ла манча, Испания. Его родителей звали Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO и Estevainha Peres. Он женился на женщине по имени Constanza Osorio около 1150 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. в местности кастилла и леoн, Испания. Он умер в 1185 г. в местности монфорт, монфорт дель сид, аликант, комунидад веиленсяна, Испания в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Fernando Rodríguez de Castro "el Castellano" nació en 1125, en Castilla-La Mancha, España como hijo de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO y Estevainha Peres. Se casó con Constanza Osorio aproximadamente 1150. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Su ocupación registrada era aristócrata, noble, caballero, mayordomo real castellano (1125-1185), señor de la casa de castro. en Castilla y León, España. Murió en 1185, en Monforte, Monforte del Cid, Alicante, Comunidad Valenciana, España, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en España.
